arabic_challenge: /v/ does not exist in standard Arabic (substituted with /f/). /θ/ and /ð/ exist in Arabic but many dialects drop them. The /ʊ/ vs /uː/ distinction is not meaningful in Arabic.

isolation.instructions: Both: top teeth on lower lip. /f/ = air only, no voice. /v/ = same position + voice vibrates.
isolation.instructions_ar: /f/ و /v/: الأسنان العلوية على الشفة السفلية. /f/ = هواء فقط. /v/ = نفس الوضع + اهتزاز.

sentence_practice: I have five very good friends.
I never visit on Friday evenings.
daily_drills.record_this: I have five friends. Every Friday we visit my favorite place. I love it very much.
daily_drills.self_check: Put your hand on your throat: /f/ = no vibration. /v/ = vibration. Can you feel the difference?

isolation.instructions: Tongue tip between your teeth (sticks out slightly!). /θ/ (think) = air only. /ð/ (this) = voice vibrates. These exist in Arabic (ث and ذ) but many speakers drop them.
isolation.instructions_ar: طرف اللسان بين الأسنان. /θ/ = مثل ث (ثلاثة). /ð/ = مثل ذ (ذلك). موجودين بالعربي!

sentence_practice: I think this is the third thing.
They went there together on Thursday.
My mother and father are both thankful.
daily_drills.record_this: I think three things: this, that, and the other. My mother thanks them both.
daily_drills.self_check: Is your tongue BETWEEN your teeth? If you can't see your tongue tip in a mirror, it's not far enough forward.

isolation.instructions: /s/ = tongue behind upper teeth (not between!), air flows through. /z/ = same position + voice.
isolation.instructions_ar: /s/ = اللسان خلف الأسنان العلوية، هواء يمر. /z/ = نفس الوضع + اهتزاز.

sentence_practice: This is his sister. She is busy.
Please, the price is easy to see.
daily_drills.record_this: This is easy. His sister has six prizes. Please close your eyes because it is a surprise.
daily_drills.self_check: Important: The letter 's' is sometimes pronounced /z/! Words like 'is, his, was, eyes, please' all use /z/ not /s/.

isolation.instructions: /ɑː/ (father, car) = mouth wide open, tongue low and back, like saying 'ahhh' at the doctor. /oʊ/ (go, no) = lips round, moves from 'o' to 'oo'.
isolation.instructions_ar: /ɑː/ = الفم مفتوح واسع (مثل عند الدكتور: آآآ). /oʊ/ = الشفاه مدوّرة، يتحرك من o إلى oo.

sentence_practice: I know my father is not home.
Go to the car. It's not far from the park.
Both of us go home when it's cold.
daily_drills.record_this: My father has an old car. I don't know if he is home. Go to the park — it's not far. I hope it's not too hot or cold.
daily_drills.self_check: For /ɑː/: is your mouth as open as at the dentist? For /oʊ/: do your lips move from open-round to more closed?

isolation.instructions: /ʊ/ (book, good) = SHORT, lips slightly rounded, relaxed. /uː/ (food, too) = LONG, lips very rounded and pushed forward.
isolation.instructions_ar: /ʊ/ = قصير، الشفاه مدوّرة قليلاً ومسترخية. /uː/ = طويل، الشفاه مدوّرة جداً ومدفوعة للأمام.
practice: oooo (long, lips pushed forward like a kiss)

sentence_practice: The food is good. The cook should look.
Put the book in the room. It's too full.
I could go to school soon.
daily_drills.record_this: I put the book on the table and look at the food. It's good. The room is full. I should go to school soon — it's too far to walk.
daily_drills.self_check: For /ʊ/: short and relaxed (think 'book' — quick!). For /uː/: long and your lips push forward like blowing a candle.

challenge_sentences: I think five of them live together.
My father never visits on Thursday.
The food is very good — not too hot.
This book is full of useful things.
They have three brothers and six sisters.
daily_drills.record_this: I think this food is very good. My father has five books — they are full of very useful things. I never visit the school on Thursday because it is too far.
daily_drills.self_check: Focus on: /v/ in 'very/visit/five', /θ/ in 'think/three/Thursday', /ð/ in 'this/they/together', and long vs short vowels.

test_yourself.passage: My father thinks this food is very good. He has five favorite things: his family, his car, good books, cold weather, and football on Thursday. Three of us go to school every day. The room is not very full — there is food for everyone. I am very thankful for everything.

sounds_to_check./f/ vs /v/: five, favorite, very, football, everything, for, food, full, family
sounds_to_check./θ/ vs /ð/: thinks, this, three, Thursday, thankful — the, there, everything
sounds_to_check./ʊ/ vs /uː/: good, books, football, full — food, school, room, too

scoring_guide.90_100: All fricatives distinct. Vowel lengths clearly different.
scoring_guide.70_89: Good overall. Occasional /f/-/v/ or vowel length confusion.
scoring_guide.50_69: /v/ still sounds like /f/ in some words. Vowel lengths unclear.
